_note: Pinned sample reading for /personal-year-card/sample. Maya, 3/3, 2026 (Lovers Bearing, Tower year). Curated showcase copy — edit freely; it does not regenerate.

sections.framing: Maya, this is your Tower year. The card that meets you in 2026 is the one that brings down what was never going to hold, and the twelve months below carry that pressure in their own shapes. What follows is the Tower's own reading. Read it knowing it's your year, not a weather report for someone else's.
reflections: The Tower runs against your Lovers grain, taking down what you never got to choose. Where in your life is the clench you already feel, the thing that looks fine on paper and keeps you braced?
Your year reckons before it begins, closing the World in May before the Fool opens the door in June. What are you hoping is finished before you'd let yourself start again?
Testing sits out entirely this year. If nothing is asking you to prove what you're made of, what would you do with a year that only asks you to clear and then rebuild?
February and August are your only slow, unknowing months in a year built for clarity. What is it you keep sensing but haven't let yourself say out loud yet?
December hands you back the Lovers, your own card, the choice from your actual center. Who or what do you already suspect you'll be choosing when the dust has settled?
sections.stagesAndArc:
Your year opens deep in Reckoning and works backward toward the beginning. January through May move through the accounting cards: the Star returning something that went away, the Moon in February with its half-seen pull, the Sun in March, Judgement in April closing the case, the World in May marking a thing as finished. Then June turns the whole thing over. From the Fool in June through December's Lovers, you're in Initiation, gathering the equipment again, the first moves and unmapped tries, a life getting reassembled after the clearing. The year runs the story in reverse: you reckon first, then you begin.

Testing is the stage this year never enters. Not one month lands there. Strength, the Hermit, the Wheel, Justice, the Hanged One, Death, Temperance, all of it sits out of reach in 2026. That's the hard middle, the place that asks what you're made of when the equipment is worn and breaking. The Tower will feel like plenty on its own, and it is, but the year isn't asking you to be tested in that grinding, drawn-out way. It's asking you to let something fall and then start rebuilding, not to endure a long siege. The collapse is quick. The work after it is fresh ground, not trial.
sections.elementWeather: Air leads your year, four months of it, the element of your own Bearing, clustered in the summer when June, July, and December put choosing and beginning back in your hands. Water is the thin one, only February and August. That means the year gives you very little space to sit in the murk and feel your way through slowly. February's Moon and August's High Priestess are nearly the only months that let you not-know on purpose. Everything around them wants clarity, action, a verdict. When those two water months come, protect them. They're where you get to feel something before you're asked to name it.
sections.bearingMeetsYear:
Your Bearing is the Lovers, the part of you that chooses with your whole self showing, that feels the difference in your body between the choices that settle you and the ones that keep you slightly clenched. That's air: choosing, alignment, being known and not turned away. The Tower is fire, and it runs against your grain. Where the Lovers waits to feel your whole self agree, the Tower doesn't wait for agreement. It takes the wall down and lets you find out afterward what you stood on. Picture a week where a thing you'd been holding up with both hands finally drops, and the relief and the loss arrive in the same breath. The part of you that likes to choose on purpose will want to have chosen this. You didn't. It fell anyway.

The arithmetic is quieter than it sounds. The Tower is your Lovers carried ten steps forward, the same instinct for what's true pushed all the way to where the false thing can't stand anymore. What you do best, feeling the clench and knowing something doesn't fit, is exactly what a Tower year needs. And December returns you to yourself: your last month of the year is the Lovers again, your own Bearing. The year that started by knocking down the cage ends by handing you back the thing you've always been, the choice made from your actual center. You close 2026 as you.
